original_image_tags: A single page of aged, off-white paper with two hole-punch marks at the top center. The paper shows significant bleed-through of text from the reverse side (which appears to be typed upside down). Faint, partially legible letterhead text is visible at the top.
A circular, embossed seal or stamp is partially visible in the upper right corner. The words "FEDERAL BUREAU OF INVESTIGATION" are legible around the perimeter.
At the bottom of the page, there are several stamps and a handwritten note in pink/red ink, oriented upright relative to the page.

elements.description: One circular stamp or seal located in the upper right corner. Below it is a line of text, followed by a horizontal rule. A second circular stamp or seal is partially visible and overlapping the first one at the bottom right corner.
A red stamp located near the bottom center of the page. The stamp contains the text 'DEC 5 3:53 PM'. Below this is another line of text: 'DEC 5 3:18 PM'.
A blue stamp located near the bottom center of the page, overlapping the red stamp. The stamp contains the text 'DEC 5 3:53 PM'. Below this is another line of text: 'DEC 5 3:18 PM'.

judge.reason: Description B quotes specific, different verbatim text for stamps ('DEC 5 3:53 PM', 'DEC 5 3:18 PM') that is not present in Description A, which describes different stamp content and placement.
